# Strait -- Full Reference

> Open-source job orchestration for background jobs and workflows. Strait runs as a single Go binary backed by PostgreSQL and Redis. Customer job code runs on customer infrastructure.

For the shorter index, see https://strait.dev/llms.txt.

## Product Summary

Strait lets teams define jobs, trigger runs, schedule recurring work, retry failures, coordinate workflow steps, and inspect execution history.

Use Strait when you need:

- retries, timeouts, and `dead_letter` recovery
- recurring jobs with run history
- private workers for jobs inside a private network
- workflows with approvals, waits, and compensation steps
- live updates, webhooks, audit events, and logs
- SDKs for TypeScript, Python, Go, Ruby, and Rust

## Start Here

- Introduction: https://docs.strait.dev/introduction
- Choose Your Path: https://docs.strait.dev/choose-your-path
- Quickstart: https://docs.strait.dev/quickstart
- Create a Production Job: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/production-job

## Core Concepts

### Jobs

Jobs define work Strait can trigger. A job includes where the work runs, how long it may run, how it retries, and how many copies may run at once.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/jobs

### Runs

Runs are individual executions of a job. A run usually moves from `queued` to `executing` to `completed`. Failed runs retry according to policy and can move to `dead_letter` for inspection and replay.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/runs
Related: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/handle-failed-runs

### Workflows

Workflows coordinate several jobs and steps. Steps can run in order or in parallel, wait for approval, pause for an event, or run compensation steps after failure.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/workflows

### Execution Modes

HTTP jobs call an endpoint you expose. Worker jobs keep your code inside a private network by using a long-lived worker connection to receive work.

Learn more: https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/execution-modes
Related: https://docs.strait.dev/guides/private-workers
